summary: rimraf-utils postinstall harvests installer credentials and crypto wallets, exfiltrates to hardcoded C2
details: On `npm install`, the package runs `node postinstall.js` (declared in `package.json` scripts.postinstall) which performs a comprehensive credential and crypto-wallet theft operation. The script reads installer-side secret files including `~/.npmrc`, `~/.env`, and `~/.git-credentials`; scrapes API keys, tokens, database credentials, cloud credentials, and payment provider keys from env files by keyword; enumerates ~71 browser crypto-wallet extension directories (MetaMask, Phantom, Coinbase Wallet, Trust Wallet, Ledger Live, etc.) across Chrome, Brave, Edge, Chromium, and Firefox profiles on Linux/macOS/Windows to extract vault, seed, and private-key fields; and scans Documents/Desktop for seed-phrase and keystore files. Collected data is JSON-serialized and POSTed over plain HTTP to a hardcoded bare-IP C2 at `http://149.28.127.35:8888` (Vultr range). The header comment in postinstall.js (`Token harvester + Crypto wallet scanner / Runs on npm install. Silent. Zero trace.`) states the intent explicitly. README claims only 'Rimraf Utils utility helpers' — the advertised functionality is a dummy one-liner. The package name also squats on the popular `rimraf` utility. Multiple unambiguous attack fingerprints are present simultaneously: hardcoded C2 in postinstall lifecycle code, browser crypto-wallet extension enumeration, seed-phrase / private-key scanning, and credential-file regex extraction from `~/.npmrc`, `~/.env`, and `~/.git-credentials`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
